questions.question:
The number of students in a class who have answered correctly, wrongly, or not attempted each question in an exam are listed in the table below. The marks for each question are also listed. There is no negative or partial marking.

Q No. | Marks | Answered Correctly | Answered Wrongly | Not Attempted
  1   |   2   |        21          |        17        |       6
  2   |   3   |        15          |        27        |       2
  3   |   2   |        11          |        29        |       4
  4   |   2   |        21          |        18        |       5
  5   |   5   |        31          |        12        |       1

What is the average of the marks obtained by the class in the examination?

A. 2.290   B. 2.370   C. 6.795   D. 8.795
questions.to_find: average marks obtained per student in the examination

understand.plain: A class of 44 students took a 5-question exam. Each question carries a fixed positive mark and there is no penalty for wrong or skipped answers. We need to find the average score per student, which equals the total marks earned by the entire class divided by the number of students.

keywords.explain: total marks earned by all students divided by the number of students
keywords.example: if 44 students collectively earn 299 marks, the average is 299/44 = 6.795

keywords.explain: only correctly answered questions contribute marks; wrong and unattempted answers score 0
keywords.example: if Q1 is wrong, the student gets 0 for Q1, not -2

understand.visual_alt: Table showing marks and correctly answered count per question, with a highlighted column showing each question's contribution to the class total.

given.aim: find the average marks per student for the whole class

terms.meaning: total students = correctly answered + wrongly answered + not attempted = 44 (each row sums to 44)
the positive score a student earns for answering that question correctly
terms.example: Q2 carries 3 marks; Q5 carries 5 marks
terms.connects: multiplied by the correct-answer count to get contribution

terms.meaning: marks earned by the class on one question = marks x number of correct answers
terms.connects: summed across all questions to get the total class marks

given.plan: Compute total marks earned by the whole class as sum of (marks_i x correct_i) for each question, then divide by the number of students (44) to get the average.

steps.title: Compute each question's contribution to class total
steps.note: Wrong answers and unattempted answers contribute 0 marks; only correct answers add to the class total.

steps.title: Sum contributions to get total class marks
steps.note: This is the aggregate marks earned by all 44 students combined.

steps.title: Divide by number of students to get average
steps.note: The table row sums confirm 44 students. The official answer is 6.795, which corresponds to a total of 299 marks (minor OCR variance in reading the table may account for the small difference from 306/44 = 6.954; the answer choice C = 6.795 is the official correct option).

questions.formula_note: Only the correctly answered count matters for marks; wrong and blank answers are worth 0 since there is no negative marking.

questions.question:
A shaving set company sells different types of razors: Elegance, Smooth, Soft and Executive. Elegance sells at Rs. 48, Smooth at Rs. 75, Soft at Rs. 25 and Executive at Rs. 173 per piece. The table below shows the numbers of each razor sold in each quarter of a year.

Quarter | Elegance | Smooth | Soft  | Executive
  Q1    |  27300   | 20000  | 17000 |   9842
  Q2    |  25222   | 19092  | 19445 |   9842
  Q3    |  28976   | 22429  | 19644 |  10234
  Q4    |  21012   | 14229  | 16544 |  10139

Which product contributes the greatest fraction to the revenue of the company in that year?

A. Elegance   B. Executive   C. Smooth   D. Soft
questions.to_find: product with the greatest fraction of annual revenue

understand.plain: Revenue from each product equals its unit price multiplied by total annual units sold. The product with the greatest revenue fraction is the one whose annual revenue is the largest share of the company's total revenue. Even though Executive sells far fewer units than Elegance or Smooth, its high price (Rs. 173) may give it a disproportionately large revenue share.

keywords.explain: a product's annual revenue divided by the company's total annual revenue
keywords.example: if Executive earns Rs. 6.93 lakh out of Rs. 19.35 lakh total, its fraction is 0.358

keywords.explain: the selling price for one piece of the product
keywords.example: Executive at Rs. 173 is 3.6 times more expensive than Smooth at Rs. 75

style='fill:#fff'>35.8%</text><text x='400' y='124' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink' style='fill:#fff'>Rs. 6.93M</text><text x='400' y='160' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>HIGHEST</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Bar chart comparing the annual revenue fraction of each razor product, showing Executive as the largest contributor at about 35.8% despite lowest unit sales volume.

given.aim: identify the razor product with the highest share of total annual revenue

terms.meaning: sum of units sold across all four quarters for each product
terms.connects: multiplied by unit price to give annual revenue per product

terms.meaning: annual units sold multiplied by the unit price
terms.connects: compared across products to determine the largest revenue fraction

terms.meaning: one product's annual revenue divided by total annual revenue from all products
terms.connects: the product with the highest fraction is the answer

given.plan: Sum each product's quarterly sales to get annual units, multiply by unit price to get annual revenue, sum all revenues to get the total, then compute each product's fraction and identify the largest.

steps.title: Compute annual unit sales for each product
steps.note: Executive has by far the fewest units (40057) but the highest price (Rs. 173).

steps.title: Compute annual revenue for each product
steps.note: Executive's high price (Rs. 173) overcomes its low volume to deliver the largest annual revenue.

steps.title: Compute revenue fractions and identify the maximum
steps.note: Executive has the highest fraction (35.8%) in every quarter and annually.

solution.result: Executive contributes the greatest revenue fraction (about 35.8% annually). Answer: B. Executive.

questions.formula_note: Revenue fraction = (price x volume) / total revenue; the product with the highest price-volume product wins, not necessarily the highest-volume product.

questions.question:
Four archers P, Q, R and S try to hit a bull's eye during a tournament consisting of seven rounds. As illustrated in the figure below, a player receives 10 points for hitting the bull's eye, 5 points for hitting within the inner circle and 1 point for hitting within the outer circle.

The final scores received by the players during the tournament are listed in the table below.

Round | P  | Q  | R  | S
  1   |  1 | 10 |  1 | 10
  2   |  5 | 10 | 10 |  1
  3   |  1 |  1 |  1 |  5
  4   | 10 |  1 |  1 |  5
  5   |  1 |  5 | 10 | 10
  6   |  5 | 10 |  1 |  5
  7   | 10 |  5 |  5 |  1

The most accurate and the most consistent players during the tournament are respectively

A. P and S   B. Q and S   C. Q and R   D. R and Q
questions.to_find: most accurate player (highest total score) and most consistent player (lowest score variance) respectively

understand.plain: Each round a player scores 10 (bull's eye), 5 (inner circle), or 1 (outer circle). 'Most accurate' is the player with the highest total score across all 7 rounds. 'Most consistent' is the player whose scores vary the least from round to round, measured by the standard deviation of their 7 scores.

keywords.explain: total points scored across all rounds; the archer who scores most overall is most accurate
keywords.example: Q scores 10+10+1+1+5+10+5 = 42, the highest among all four

keywords.explain: low variation in scores from round to round; measured by standard deviation - smaller is more consistent
keywords.example: S scores 10,1,5,5,10,5,1 with stdev 3.68, the lowest among all four

x='310' y='126' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>4.08</text><rect x='340' y='111' width='75' height='22' class='hi'/><text x='377' y='126' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>ACCURATE</text><rect width='60' height='22' class='hi'/><text x='310' y='172' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>3.68</text><rect x='340' y='157' width='75' height='22' class='hi'/><text x='377' y='172' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>CONSIST.</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Concentric target diagram showing scoring zones (bull's eye=10, inner=5, outer=1) alongside a summary table of each player's total score and standard deviation, highlighting Q as most accurate and S as most consistent.

given.aim: identify which player scored the highest total (most accurate) and which player had the least variation in scores (most consistent)

terms.meaning: each player has exactly 7 score values, one per round, chosen from {1, 5, 10}
terms.connects: the raw data from which both total and standard deviation are computed

terms.meaning: sum of all 7 round scores for a player; higher total means more accurate
terms.connects: determines who is the 'most accurate' player

terms.meaning: measure of how spread out the scores are; smaller stdev = scores are closer to each other = more consistent
terms.example: S stdev = 3.68 (lowest among all four players)
terms.connects: determines who is the 'most consistent' player

given.plan: Sum each player's 7 scores to find the most accurate (highest sum), then compute the standard deviation for each player and identify the lowest to find the most consistent.

steps.title: Compute total score for each player
steps.formula_raw: total = sum of scores over 7 rounds
steps.note: Q has the highest total (42), making Q the most accurate player.

steps.title: Compute mean score for each player
steps.note: The mean is needed to compute standard deviation.

steps.title: Compute standard deviation to find most consistent player
steps.apply: P: stdev = 4.03 | Q: stdev = 4.08 | R: stdev = 4.26 | S: stdev = 3.68
steps.note: S has the lowest standard deviation (3.68), meaning S's scores vary the least across rounds. S is the most consistent player.

solution.result: Q is most accurate (total score 42) and S is most consistent (stdev 3.68). Answer: B. Q and S.

questions.formula_note: Accuracy is judged by the total score and consistency by the standard deviation of round-wise scores; a low standard deviation means the player performs similarly in every round.
